title: hedysarum boreale subsp. boreale
intro: Hedysarum boreale subsp. boreale, also known as the northern sweetvetch or Bering sweetvetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the arctic and alpine regions of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Hedysarum boreale subsp. boreale include its ability to help lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Hedysarum boreale subsp. boreale can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
